title: cercocarpus montanus var. macrourus
intro: Cercocarpus montanus var. macrourus, also known as mountain mahogany, is a variety of shrub or small tree that belongs to the family Rosaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for treating various health issues. The health benefits of mountain mahogany include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and soothe digestive issues. Herbal preparations of mountain mahogany typically involve drying the leaves and stems to make a tea or infusing the plant in oil for topical use.
